Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --api-sock process contract with the documented macOS default-path equivalent.
Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --boot-timer process contract for the supported aarch64 guest boot timer.
Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --config-file process startup contract while delegating section semantics to their capability families.
Audit the Firecracker v1.16 executable argument --describe-snapshot.
Audit the Firecracker v1.16 executable argument --enable-pci.

Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --id Unicode-alphanumeric identity and UTF-8 byte-bound contract.
Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --level process logger configuration contract.
Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --log-path process logger sink contract.
Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --metadata process-local MMDS initialization contract.
Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --metrics-path process metrics sink contract.
Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --mmds-size-limit inheritance and non-negative usize contract.
Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --module process logger prefix-filter contract.
Provide the Firecracker v1.16 --no-api prerequisite, readiness, execution, and no-socket contract.
Audit the Firecracker v1.16 executable argument --no-seccomp.

Audit the pinned non-Swagger runtime route DELETE /drives/{drive_id}.
Audit the pinned non-Swagger runtime route DELETE /network-interfaces/{iface_id}.
Audit the pinned non-Swagger runtime route DELETE /pmem/{id}.
Audit kernel/initrd/rootfs loading, boot arguments, DeviceTree construction, boot cache behavior, and startup failure ordering.
Audit arm CPU configuration, templates, feature state, modifier behavior, and applicable helper operations.
Audit interactions among lifecycle state, API errors, metrics/logging, security/resource ownership, devices, networking, and snapshots.
Audit entropy configuration, guest queue behavior, host randomness, limiting, wakeups, metrics, cleanup, and snapshot state.
Audit RTC, VMClock, VMGenID, PVTime applicability, interrupts, mutable state, clone behavior, metrics, and restore ordering.
Audit serial default stdout, configured output, stdin/RX, TX/RX queues, limiting, metrics, buffering, cleanup, and in-flight restore.
Audit failure-atomic runtime block, pmem, and network attach/remove with guest preconditions, rollback, metrics, and cleanup.

Lifecycle v2 gates Proceed on an atomic bounded startup batch with strict no-follow manifests, typed SCM_RIGHTS file descriptors, anchored one-session mutable-directory bookmarks, exact session/batch/sequence/identity/access validation, and one-time redacted registry ownership. Contained config, metadata, kernel, and initrd consumers adopt exact read-only descriptors without reopening tagged paths; signed normal-bundle proof covers no-API/API use, pathname replacement, failure-atomic claims, redaction, and real guest boot. Remaining consumers, dynamic brokerage/hard revocation, vmnet authority, and resource-specific mutation/cleanup remain feasible under #1351.

The fixed separately signed App Sandbox plus Hypervisor worker has a default-close standard/lifecycle/grant descriptor allowlist, authenticated lifecycle v2, mandatory atomic startup-grant acknowledgment, a locked private namespace, and exact contained consumption of granted startup config, metadata, kernel, and initrd files. Remaining jailer limits, credentials, daemonization, seccomp, other consumer resources, network, and deployment outcomes require explicit macOS equivalents under #1351.

The fixed launcher owns one dynamically validated worker through exact-sequence lifecycle and grant channels with nonblocking signal/child observation, one absolute grant deadline, failure-atomic staging and exact multi-file claims, synchronized pending-authority invalidation on cancellation/disconnect, structured readiness/terminal state, replacement-safe namespaces, both grant-bearing crash-order cleanup, bounded both-killed recovery, redaction, and signed noninterchangeable concurrent authority. Dynamic-resource races and malicious same-container sibling isolation remain feasible under #1351.
Audit pause/resume, snapshot-ready quiescence, auxiliary work ownership, state admission, and fine-grained failure behavior.
Audit multi-vCPU creation, owner-thread execution, PSCI power transitions, interrupts, timers, and terminal guest outcomes.
Audit balloon inflate/deflate, deflate-on-OOM, statistics transitions, hinting/reporting, host reclaim, metrics, and snapshot state.
Audit virtio-mem sizing, block lifecycle, guest/HVF mappings, public accounting/deletion, rollback, metrics, and snapshot state.
Audit machine memory sizing, huge-page policy, mapping/protection, dirty tracking, bounds, and failure atomicity.
Audit MMDS v1/v2 tokens, complete TCP/session behavior, reassembly/retransmission/timeouts, interface policy, isolation, and snapshots.
Audit virtio-net queues, vmnet properties/callbacks/batching, egress policy, limiting, metrics, connectivity, cleanup, PCI, and snapshot state.
Audit logger configuration, records, filtering, rate limits, delivery/loss, panic/fatal behavior, redaction, isolation, and cleanup.
Audit metrics schema and every request/action/device/failure producer, timing, periodic/explicit/terminal flush, loss, backpressure, and isolation.
Provide CLI parsing, configuration-file/no-API startup, API socket ownership, readiness, and process startup ordering.

validation.anchor: signed API and no-api startup validation
API, config-file, no-api, failure, and concurrent process validation

capabilities.summary: Audit instance identity validation plus help, version, snapshot-version, and snapshot-description output semantics.
Provide fatal and nonfatal signal handling, exit classes, descriptor-table behavior, terminal cleanup, and repeatable process ownership.

implementation.anchor: signal, exit, fd-table, and ProcessCleanup ownership

validation.anchor: signal, exit, socket cleanup, and repeatable ownership validation

capabilities.summary: Audit Diff snapshots, dirty tracking, File and UFFD-equivalent memory backends, cancellation, bounds, and security.
Audit snapshot editor, rebase, inspection, version reporting, failure behavior, and applicable architecture semantics.
Audit Full snapshot create/load admission, quiescence, publication, validation, cleanup, resume, and guest continuation.
Audit snapshot state and restore for multiple vCPUs, writable/multiple drives, optional devices, MMDS, interrupts, timers, and serial.
Audit network/vsock restore overrides, clone identity/time rules, artifact/host portability, versioning, and cross-process restore.
Audit applicable API availability, stability, request/failure information, startup, and operational specification outcomes.
Audit portable boot/overhead/CPU/network/storage/latency, resource-density, and telemetry-loss outcomes separately from AWS/Linux host promises.
Audit virtio-block Sync/Async behavior, vhost-user, cache/flush/root semantics, limiting, metrics, cleanup, and snapshot state.
Audit pmem root/direct mapping, persistence/flush, limiting, dirty policy, cleanup, PCI activation, and snapshot state.
Audit packaging, help/version, input/output authority, errors, cleanup, and architecture applicability for all pinned public tools.
Audit optional PCI host bridge, config/BAR layout, virtio-pci, MSI/MSI-X, limits, and MMIO coexistence.
Audit virtio-MMIO discovery, feature negotiation, queues, interrupts, reset, limits, and device activation.
Audit live vsock routing, credit flow, events, deadlines, errors, metrics, resource authority, and cleanup.
Audit vsock snapshot state, restore UDS override, TRANSPORT_RESET, post-restore RX gating, and port/state preservation.
